reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between x, v, and t. Increasing t might help confirm if x is indeed v*t + x0.
Increasing t confirmed the linear relationship. Now, I will increase v to observe its effect.

The value of x has consistently increased by multiples of v when t remains constant, suggesting the equation x = v*t + x0. Since increasing v or t individually results in predictable changes to x, we can conclude the equation.
